newsBriefing: In a day defined by high-stakes drama and significant setbacks, Kimi Antonelli made history as the youngest pole-sitter in Formula 1 history at the Chinese Grand Prix, while Team USA booked a dream World Baseball Classic semifinal clash against the Dominican Republic after a hard-fought 5-3 victory over Canada. College basketball's "Selection Sunday" countdown intensified as Jaden Bradley’s last-second buzzer-beater propelled No. 2 Arizona past Iowa State and into the Big 12 final, while UCLA managed to oust No. 8 Michigan State in the Big Ten despite losing star Tyler Bilodeau to a serious knee injury. The injury bug bit professional leagues even harder, with Toronto Maple Leafs captain Auston Matthews ruled out for the remainder of the NHL season due to a torn MCL and the Golden State Warriors losing four more players in a single night as their Western Conference slide continued. Amidst the playoff pushes and title chases, the coaching carousel began to turn with Cincinnati moving to fire Wes Miller after five seasons without a tournament appearance, and the WNBA faced an urgent Monday deadline to finalize a new CBA to avoid impacting its upcoming season.
